verses.latin_vulgate: Fili mi, si spoponderis pro amico tuo,  defixisti apud extraneum manum tuam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭נִי אִם־עָרַ֣בְתָּ לְרֵעֶ֑ךָ תָּקַ֖עְתָּ לַזָּ֣ר כַּפֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,

verses.latin_vulgate: illaqueatus es verbis oris tui,  et captus propriis sermonibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נוֹקַ֥שְׁתָּ בְאִמְרֵי־פִ֑יךָ נִ֝לְכַּ֗דְתָּ בְּאִמְרֵי־פִֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fac ergo quod dico, fili mi, et temetipsum libera,  quia incidisti in manum proximi tui.  Discurre, festina, suscita amicum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲשֵׂ֨ה זֹ֥את אֵפ֪וֹא ׀ בְּנִ֡י וְֽהִנָּצֵ֗ל כִּ֘י־בָ֤אתָ בְכַף־רֵעֶ֑ךָ לֵ֥ךְ הִ֝תְרַפֵּ֗ס וּרְהַ֥ב רֵעֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend; go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dederis somnum oculis tuis,  nec dormitent palpebræ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתֵּ֣ן שֵׁנָ֣ה לְעֵינֶ֑יךָ וּ֝תְנוּמָ֗ה לְעַפְעַפֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eruere quasi damula de manu,  et quasi avis de manu aucupis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭נָּצֵל כִּצְבִ֣י מִיָּ֑ד וּ֝כְצִפּ֗וֹר מִיַּ֥ד יָקֽוּשׁ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand of the hunter, and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade ad formicam, o piger,  et considera vias ejus, et disce sapientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵֽךְ־אֶל־נְמָלָ֥ה עָצֵ֑ל רְאֵ֖ה דְרָכֶ֣יהָ וַחֲכָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ cum non habeat ducem,  nec præceptorem, nec principem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֵֽין־לָ֥הּ קָצִ֗ין שֹׁטֵ֥ר וּמֹשֵֽׁל׃
verses.king_james: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,

verses.latin_vulgate: parat in æstate cibum sibi,  et congregat in messe quod comedat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תָּכִ֣ין בַּקַּ֣יִץ לַחְמָ֑הּ אָגְרָ֥ה בַ֝קָּצִ֗יר מַאֲכָלָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Usquequo, piger, dormies ?  quando consurges e somno tuo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־מָתַ֖י עָצֵ֥ל ׀ תִּשְׁכָּ֑ב מָ֝תַ֗י תָּק֥וּם מִשְּׁנָתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

verses.latin_vulgate: Paululum dormies, paululum dormitabis,  paululum conseres manus ut dormias ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְעַ֣ט שֵׁ֭נוֹת מְעַ֣ט תְּנוּמ֑וֹת מְעַ֓ט ׀ חִבֻּ֖ק יָדַ֣יִם לִשְׁכָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

verses.latin_vulgate: et veniet tibi quasi viator egestas,  et pauperies quasi vir armatus.  Si vero impiger fueris, veniet ut fons messis tua,  et egestas longe fugiet a te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָֽא־כִמְהַלֵּ֥ךְ רֵאשֶׁ֑ךָ וּ֝מַחְסֹרְךָ֗ כְּאִ֣ישׁ מָגֵֽן׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo apostata, vir inutilis, graditur ore perverso ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָדָ֣ם בְּ֭לִיַּעַל אִ֣ישׁ אָ֑וֶן ה֝וֹלֵ֗ךְ עִקְּשׁ֥וּת פֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: annuit oculis, terit pede, digito loquitur,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֹרֵ֣ץ בְּ֭עֵינָו מֹלֵ֣ל בְּרַגְלָ֑ו מֹ֝רֶ֗ה בְּאֶצְבְּעֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers;

verses.latin_vulgate: pravo corde machinatur malum,  et omni tempore jurgia seminat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֥הְפֻּכ֨וֹת ׀ בְּלִבּ֗וֹ חֹרֵ֣שׁ רָ֣ע בְּכׇל־עֵ֑ת (מדנים) [מִדְיָנִ֥ים] יְשַׁלֵּֽחַ׃
verses.king_james: Frowardness is in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Huic extemplo veniet perditio sua,  et subito conteretur, nec habebit ultra medicinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֗ן פִּ֭תְאֹם יָב֣וֹא אֵיד֑וֹ פֶּ֥תַע יִ֝שָּׁבֵ֗ר וְאֵ֣ין מַרְפֵּֽא׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sex sunt quæ odit Dominus,  et septimum detestatur anima ejus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֶׁשׁ־הֵ֭נָּה שָׂנֵ֣א יְהֹוָ֑ה וְ֝שֶׁ֗בַע (תועבות) [תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת] נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: These six things doth the Lord hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him:

verses.latin_vulgate: oculos sublimes, linguam mendacem,  manus effundentes innoxium sanguinem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינַ֣יִם רָ֭מוֹת לְשׁ֣וֹן שָׁ֑קֶר וְ֝יָדַ֗יִם שֹׁפְכ֥וֹת דָּם־נָקִֽי׃
verses.king_james: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,

verses.latin_vulgate: cor machinans cogitationes pessimas,  pedes veloces ad currendum in malum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֗ב חֹ֭רֵשׁ מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת אָ֑וֶן רַגְלַ֥יִם מְ֝מַהֲר֗וֹת לָר֥וּץ לָרָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief,

verses.latin_vulgate: proferentem mendacia testem fallacem,  et eum qui seminat inter fratres discordias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָפִ֣יחַ כְּ֭זָבִים עֵ֣ד שָׁ֑קֶר וּמְשַׁלֵּ֥חַ מְ֝דָנִ֗ים בֵּ֣ין אַחִֽים׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conserva, fili mi, præcepta patris tui,  et ne dimittas legem matris tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נְצֹ֣ר בְּ֭נִי מִצְוַ֣ת אָבִ֑יךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝טֹּ֗שׁ תּוֹרַ֥ת אִמֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:

verses.latin_vulgate: Liga ea in corde tuo jugiter,  et circumda gutturi tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קׇשְׁרֵ֣ם עַל־לִבְּךָ֣ תָמִ֑יד עׇ֝נְדֵ֗ם עַל־גַּרְגְּרֹתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ambulaveris, gradiantur tecum ;  cum dormieris, custodiant te :  et evigilans loquere cum eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּהִתְהַלֶּכְךָ֨ ׀ תַּנְחֶ֬ה אֹתָ֗ךְ בְּֽ֭שׇׁכְבְּךָ תִּשְׁמֹ֣ר עָלֶ֑יךָ וַ֝הֲקִיצ֗וֹתָ הִ֣יא תְשִׂיחֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia mandatum lucerna est, et lex lux,  et via vitæ increpatio disciplinæ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י נֵ֣ר מִ֭צְוָה וְת֣וֹרָה א֑וֹר וְדֶ֥רֶךְ חַ֝יִּ֗ים תּוֹכְח֥וֹת מוּסָֽר׃
verses.king_james: For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut custodiant te a muliere mala,  et a blanda lingua extraneæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִ֭שְׁמׇרְךָ מֵאֵ֣שֶׁת רָ֑ע מֵ֝חֶלְקַ֗ת לָשׁ֥וֹן נׇכְרִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non concupiscat pulchritudinem ejus cor tuum,  nec capiaris nutibus illius :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּחְמֹ֣ד יׇ֭פְיָהּ בִּלְבָבֶ֑ךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝קָּחֲךָ֗ בְּעַפְעַפֶּֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.

verses.latin_vulgate: pretium enim scorti vix est unius panis,  mulier autem viri pretiosam animam capit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י בְעַד־אִשָּׁ֥ה זוֹנָ֗ה עַֽד־כִּכַּ֫ר־לָ֥חֶם וְאֵ֥שֶׁת אִ֑ישׁ נֶ֖פֶשׁ יְקָרָ֣ה תָצֽוּד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid potest homo abscondere ignem in sinu suo,  ut vestimenta illius non ardeant ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיַחְתֶּ֤ה אִ֓ישׁ אֵ֬שׁ בְּחֵיק֑וֹ וּ֝בְגָדָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִשָּׂרַֽפְנָה׃
verses.king_james: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut ambulare super prunas,  ut non comburantur plantæ ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יְהַלֵּ֣ךְ אִ֭ישׁ עַל־הַגֶּחָלִ֑ים וְ֝רַגְלָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִכָּוֶֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?

verses.latin_vulgate: sic qui ingreditur ad mulierem proximi sui,  non erit mundus cum tetigerit eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֗ן הַ֭בָּא אֶל־אֵ֣שֶׁת רֵעֵ֑הוּ לֹ֥א יִ֝נָּקֶ֗ה כׇּֽל־הַנֹּגֵ֥עַ בָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: So he that goeth in to his neighbour’s wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non grandis est culpa cum quis furatus fuerit :  furatur enim ut esurientem impleat animam ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יָב֣וּזוּ לַ֭גַּנָּב כִּ֣י יִגְנ֑וֹב לְמַלֵּ֥א נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ כִּ֣י יִרְעָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Men do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry;

verses.latin_vulgate: deprehensus quoque reddet septuplum,  et omnem substantiam domus suæ tradet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭נִמְצָא יְשַׁלֵּ֣ם שִׁבְעָתָ֑יִם אֶת־כׇּל־ה֖וֹן בֵּית֣וֹ יִתֵּֽן׃
verses.king_james: But if he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem adulter est,  propter cordis inopiam perdet animam suam ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹאֵ֣ף אִשָּׁ֣ה חֲסַר־לֵ֑ב מַֽשְׁחִ֥ית נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ ה֣וּא יַעֲשֶֽׂנָּה׃
verses.king_james: But whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: turpitudinem et ignominiam congregat sibi,  et opprobrium illius non delebitur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶגַע־וְקָל֥וֹן יִמְצָ֑א וְ֝חֶרְפָּת֗וֹ לֹ֣א תִמָּחֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia zelus et furor viri  non parcet in die vindictæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־קִנְאָ֥ה חֲמַת־גָּ֑בֶר וְלֹא־יַ֝חְמ֗וֹל בְּי֣וֹם נָקָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.

verses.latin_vulgate: nec acquiescet cujusquam precibus,  nec suscipiet pro redemptione dona plurima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יִ֭שָּׂא פְּנֵ֣י כׇל־כֹּ֑פֶר וְלֹא־יֹ֝אבֶ֗ה כִּ֣י תַרְבֶּה־שֹּֽׁחַד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.
